candidates.why: Explicit separate operating D&A line above operating profit, so D&A is not embedded within the selected COGS row. However, this appears to be a company-wide operating D&A total rather than a COGS-specific D&A row, so assigning it to COGS would risk misclassification and double counting against XSGA.
Component of the separate operating D&A section, but not identified as COGS-specific. Should not be used for COGS_DA when a higher-level D&A summary exists and no functional split to COGS is shown.
Component of the separate operating D&A section, but not identified as COGS-specific. Using it for COGS_DA would be unsupported without a functional allocation.

variables.notes: Selected COGS row is 'Cost of materials and traffic charges'. The statement shows a separate same-level operating line 'Depreciation and amortisation', indicating D&A is outside the selected COGS bucket. But no row specifies the portion related to COGS versus SG&A, so no COGS-specific D&A row can be added safely.

candidates.why: Explicit separate operating D&A line above operating profit, so D&A is not embedded within the selected XSGA rows ('Salaries and personnel costs' and 'Other operating expenses'). However, it appears to be a total operating D&A line, not an SG&A-specific amount, so adding it to XSGA would risk overstating SG&A and double counting with any COGS-related D&A.
Subcomponent of the separate operating D&A section, but not labelled as SG&A/admin/sales/R&D-specific. Not appropriate to add separately to XSGA when no functional SG&A split is visible.
Subcomponent of the separate operating D&A section, but not labelled as SG&A/admin/sales/R&D-specific. Not appropriate to add separately to XSGA without a functional split.

variables.notes: The selected XSGA bucket is the combination of 'Salaries and personnel costs' and 'Other operating expenses'. A separate operating line 'Depreciation and amortisation' exists, so D&A is outside those selected rows. But the excerpt provides no SG&A-specific D&A row and no R&D-specific amortisation row above operating profit, so nothing can be added safely to XSGA_DA.
